title: quercus peduncularis subsp. hurteri
intro: Quercus peduncularis subsp. hurteri, also known as Hurter's pedunculate oak, is a subspecies of Quercus peduncularis, a type of deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus peduncularis subsp. hurteri are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, suggesting its potential use in treating conditions such as arthritis, high blood pressure, and certain infections. Quercus peduncularis subsp. hurteri can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
